confirmation.detail: inbox envelope env_c22d83d9-58e7-4731-b361-24057e02917e read back as archived

evidence: Current Canonical Inbox supports CLI submission, shell-safe submit-observation, JSON payload files, and .ai/inbox-envelopes JSON export/import. That is sufficient for agents and scripts but still too hard for a human who wants to leave a message by writing a file. The existing .ai/inbox-envelopes folder is a projection/replay substrate for full envelope JSON, not a simple human text dropbox.
proposal: Add a canonical file-drop intake such as .ai/inbox-drop/ or a configured Site-local inbox/drop/ folder. Each dropped .md or .txt file should be treated as a source candidate with provenance, parsed into a typed envelope using front matter when present and safe defaults when absent, admitted only after validation, moved or marked as admitted/rejected, and linked to the resulting envelope id. Rejections should preserve reason and original file path. The mechanism should be idempotent and should not duplicate envelopes on repeated scans.
payload.recommendation: Implement this as a first-class inbox source adapter and CLI workflow, for example narada inbox ingest-files --from <folder> --dry-run and narada inbox ingest-files --from <folder> --admit. Require dry-run output showing candidate kind, authority level, payload summary, and validation failures before mutation.
payload.summary: Humans need to be able to give Narada messages by creating ordinary text or Markdown files in a designated folder. This should not create a second inbox authority. The folder should be a governed source surface whose files are parsed, validated, admitted or rejected, and then represented as canonical typed envelopes in the Canonical Inbox.
payload.title: Canonical Inbox needs human-authored text file drop intake

promotion.note: Envelope archived; no target-zone mutation was performed.
